concerns.text: T023a (Define rubric) is listed AFTER T020, T023, T021, T022. T023 explicitly states it uses 'the rubric from T023a'. A consumer (T023) cannot precede its producer (T023a). T023a must be moved to Phase 2 or the very start of Phase 4 before any metric calculation tasks.

T034 (Execute correction) depends on T029 (Implement functions) and T028 (Implement stats). However, T034 is listed AFTER T030a/b (Sensitivity analysis) and T031 (Cost profiler). While T034 doesn't strictly depend on T030/31, the logical flow of 'Implement -> Execute -> Report' is broken. More critically, T033 (Generate final report) depends on T034's output (corrected p-values), but T033 is listed after T032. Ensure T034 precedes T033 explicitly. The current order implies T033 might run before T034 executes the correction.

T004c (Implement fallback strategy) is listed after T004a (Validate fields). The fallback strategy (T004c) logically depends on the validation logic (T004a) to know *when* to trigger the fallback. While they are in the same file, the implementation of the fallback logic must follow the definition of the validation failure condition. T004c should immediately follow T004a.

T014a (Skeleton) and T014b (Logic) are split. T015 (Deletion log priority) depends on the logic in T014b. The order T014a -> T014b -> T015 is correct, but T016 (Timeout) and T017 (Logging) are cross-cutting concerns that should ideally be integrated into the pipeline implementation (T014b) or immediately follow it as a cohesive unit. The current separation is acceptable but could be tighter.
